title: epipactis fascicularis
intro: Epipactis fascicularis, also known as the fascicled helleborine or fascicled epipactis, is a species of orchid native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and leafy stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Epipactis fascicularis are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and act as an antiviral agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent require further scientific research and validation.
